commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS=1` to the post-core update child environment and updates the existing respawn test to assert it.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests 0. Total +1 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main’s post-core spawn env lacks `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS`, and the PR’s added assertion would fail against that env shape. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair candidate: the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof for the update handoff, not a code defect.

target.title: fix(update): mark post-core child as update in progress
target.body:
## Summary
- Mark the post-core update child process with `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS=1` as well as `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1`.
- Keeps generic update-phase guards active during the fresh-process handoff after a package update, including future-config warning suppression paths that key off the broader update-in-progress flag.
- Adds regression coverage to the post-core respawn test so the handoff environment carries both update markers.

## Test Plan
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts -t "respawns into the updated package root before running post-update tasks"`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD^..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof
Before the fix, the focused regression test failed because the post-core child environment did not include `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS`:

```text
AssertionError: expected undefined to be '1'
```

After the fix, the same focused test and the full update CLI test file pass, proving package-update post-core respawns now carry the generic update-in-progress marker in addition to the post-core-specific marker.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
